page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, utilizing a dedicated CPU allocation. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes 128 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% of results at 2865.09. Multi-core CPU performance is average, scoring 11559.23 in stress-ng. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance, yielding 553.27 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 46.79 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic and compression tasks also perform within average ranges, with gzip single-threaded compression reaching 28.36 GB/sec and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression at 38.08 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance handles 8.77 million Redis SET operations per second.

The c2d-standard-8 offers a balanced resource density for compute-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. Its dedicated AMD EPYC cores provide predictable performance, making this instance a cost-effective choice for steady-state workloads compared to burstable alternatives. It is suitable for web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations. However, workloads requiring high-speed local storage or massive memory-to-CPU ratios may find the lack of local disks and the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio limiting.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-8 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. Built with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core, it utilizes a dedicated CPU allocation. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average range. It achieves 8.77 million Redis SET operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 10.68 GB/sec. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose compute tasks, software compilation, and web serving workloads that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-8 is an x86_64 instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for compute-bound workloads.
tagline: An AMD EPYC powered instance with dedicated cores and balanced memory, optimized for predictable compute workloads and web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Delivers 8.77 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and general compute tasks
